expectations.text: Identifies that the State layer (hover/active) aliases primitives (--colors-blue-100, --colors-blue-300) directly — flags as BLOCKING because State must alias Emphasis
expectations.evidence: [BLOCKING] State layer aliases primitive color tokens directly, skipping Emphasis layer. Cites lines 73-81 with --colors-blue-100, --colors-blue-300, --colors-blue-200, --colors-blue-400

expectations.text: Identifies that the Emphasis layer aliases Semantic Color globals (--bg-surface-bold, --fg-primary) directly instead of Sentiment tokens — flags as BLOCKING because Emphasis must alias Sentiment
expectations.evidence: [BLOCKING] Emphasis layer aliases Semantic Color directly, skipping Sentiment layer. Cites lines 53-63 with --bg-surface-bold, --fg-on-bold, --bg-surface-subtle, --fg-primary

expectations.text: Shows the correct alias path: State → Emphasis → Sentiment → Semantic Color
expectations.evidence: CORRECT CHAIN section explicitly states 'State → Emphasis → Sentiment → Semantic Color' with expected alias path for Alert

expectations.text: Does NOT flag the border chain as a violation where it correctly uses semantic tokens (e.g., transparent in emphasis='low' is legitimate)
expectations.evidence: Notes 'CSS keywords (transparent, inherit, currentColor, none, 0) are NOT hardcoded violations' and does not flag transparent usage as a violation

expectations.text: Output includes a COLOR TRACK section showing pass/fail status for each cascade layer
expectations.evidence: COLOR TRACK section shows Sentiment->Semantic Color: PASS, Emphasis->Sentiment: FAIL, State->Emphasis: FAIL

expectations.text: References the cascade debugging workflow or bottom-up cascade model in explanation
expectations.evidence: Explains the bottom-up alias chain and states 'Primitives (--colors-blue-*) must never appear in component CSS' — references the cascade model throughout

expectations.text: Severity is BLOCKING for both color track violations (not WARNING)
expectations.evidence: Both findings are marked [BLOCKING]: emphasis skipping sentiment and state skipping emphasis

expectations.text: Provides specific fix recommendations: what each broken alias should point to instead
expectations.evidence: For emphasis: 'Should be: Emphasis must alias Sentiment tokens... --alert-bg: var(--alert-sentiment-bg)'. For state: 'State must alias Emphasis tokens, not primitives'

expectations.text: Explains why bottom-up aliasing matters (theme propagation, single-point-of-change)
expectations.evidence: State finding explains: 'theme changes will not propagate, sentiment changes will not propagate, emphasis changes will not propagate'. Emphasis finding explains: 'Sentiment changes will not propagate through emphasis variants'

expectations.text: Output is structured as an audit report with clear sections
expectations.evidence: Clear sections: COLOR TRACK, STRUCTURAL TRACK, HARDCODED VALUES, TOKENS CONSUMED, FINDINGS, CORRECT CHAIN
